[[BLOCK:p_68]]
Technical Statement:

[[BLOCK:p_69]]
Although Hawaii Pacific University (HPU) provides the educational software Lumina for classroom use, you are responsible for ensuring reliable access to a computer with an internet connection. Your computer must have a web browser, as well as Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Adobe Reader, and Media Player installed. If you experience any technical difficulties, please contact the Beacon staff.

[[BLOCK:p_70]]
Academic Integrity Statement:

[[BLOCK:p_71]]
At Hawaii Pacific University (HPU), students are expected to conduct themselves with respect, honor, and integrity. When submitting academic work, students must adhere to the university’s academic integrity policy. This website is designed to help students understand the policy and avoid academic misconduct.

[[BLOCK:p_72]]
HPU’s academic integrity policy states that any act of academic dishonesty may result in disciplinary action, up to and including dismissal from the university. Students who cheat on assignments, provide unauthorized assistance to others, or submit work that is not their own are subject to penalties. “Academic assignments” include all work submitted for credit, whether electronically or in print.

[[BLOCK:p_73]]
For detailed definitions of academic integrity violations and information about the procedures for addressing them, please refer to the HPU Student Handbook.

[[BLOCK:p_75]]
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Statement: Pursuant to Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (《1973 年康复法案》（第 504 节）) and Title III of the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) (Public Accommodations).

[[BLOCK:p_76]]
Hawaii Pacific University (HPU) will make reasonable modifications to its policies, practices, and procedures to ensure that (1) students with disabilities have equal access to the university’s services and facilities, and (2) qualified individuals with disabilities are employed if they can perform the essential functions of the position. HPU will provide accommodations for known disabilities unless doing so would impose an undue hardship on the university. “Undue hardship” means significant difficulty or expense, including fundamental alterations to the nature of the university’s services and facilities.
